title: US intelligence finds no sign Iran was rebuilding its nuclear program | ABC NEWS
description: US Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard has denied that Iran was attempting to rebuild its nuclear capabilities ahead of Operation Epic Fury. At a congressional hearing, the senior Trump official was coy on whether Tehran posed an imminent threat to Washington, while CIA Director John Ratcliffe doubled down on these claims.  ABC North America correspondent Kamin Gock says this was another instance of the Trump administration delivering conflicting messages about its motivation for strike Iran. title: Iran targets Gulf energy facilities after Israel strikes world’s largest gas field | ABC NEWS
description: Tehran has launched strikes at energy facilities of neighbouring Gulf states after Israel struck the world’s largest gas field.  The South Pars/North Dome gas field is jointly owned by Iran and Qatar. The escalation threatens to drag other Middle Eastern countries further into the conflict, as the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps' spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari labelled Iranian retaliatory strikes as a ‘firm warning’.  News of the attacks has stoked up global oil prices after Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates reported attacks on their energy sites. title: Israeli strike on South Pars gas field hits Iran's ability to keep the lights on | ABC NEWS
description: Israel has struck a massive offshore South Pars natural gas field,  shared between Qatar and Iran in the Persian Gulf. It's the largest LNG field in the world and taking it offline will have major implications for LNG and crude oil prices and supply.  Betsey Stevenson, a professor of public policy at the University of Michigan and a former member of Barack Obama's Council of Economic Advisers explains how both sides of the conflict are now inflicting economic pain as a weapon of war. title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle expected to intensify into category 5 ahead of landfall | ABC NEWS
description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is forecast to intensify into a category 5 system as it barrels towards the coast of Far North Queensland.  The storm is expected to make its first coastal crossing as a category 4 or 5, packing intense rain and damaging winds.  The Bureau of Meteorology is projecting the storm system to make two coastal more crossings in the Northern Territory and Western Australia after passing the Gulf of Carpentaria. title: Australia's red meat industry disrupted by war in the Middle East | ABC NEWS
description: As the war in the Middle East intensifies, Australia's red meat industry is being impacted. Australia exports beef and lamb to Gulf countries and North Africa by air freight, and flights are being diverted or cancelled. The CEO of Meat and Livestock Australia, Andrew Cox says despite the conflict disrupting trade, it’s an opportunity to solidify Australia's reputation as a stable and trusted partner. title: ACCC investigating allegations of anti-competitive conduct by fuel suppliers | ABC NEWS
description: The competition watchdog has announced it is investigating allegations of anti-competitive conduct by major fuel suppliers in regional and rural Australia. The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has taken the unusual step of announcing it is investigating reports about diesel availability to independent wholesalers and distributors supplying regional and rural Australia. It will probe the conduct of major players — Ampol, BP, Mobil and Viva Energy. title: PM urges Australians not to buy more fuel than needed | ABC NEWS
description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia is well-prepared for the Middle East conflict, but that he wants Australia to be "overprepared". He has urged Australians not to panic-buy fuel or take any more fuel than they need. The PM has emphasised that Australia does not have a shortage of fuel. But he says there has been a doubling of demand in some areas of the country, which has resulted in localised shortages.
